scenario: Aurora Bank runs Camunda 8.8 with 35000 payments per day. After a payment provider outage of 90 minutes is resolved, an operator needs to retry about 800 stuck process instances from the Operate list view in one batch rather than opening each instance and clicking retry one at a time on the cluster.
question: Which two steps in Operate create a selection and apply a batch operation to those stuck instances?

options.text: Open the Operations panel, type a FEEL filter that matches the stuck instances, then click apply so the broker retries every matching instance on the cluster in order
Click the incident banner on a single instance, then choose Apply to all similar instances from the dropdown so the engine fans the operation out across the partitions
Check the box next to each process instance you want to include in the selection, then choose the operation you want to apply from the controls on the list view
Drag the affected process instances into the Operations panel, then drop them onto the retry icon so the engine processes the whole group at once on the broker side

a.text: Incorrect. The Operations panel reports the status of submitted operations; it is not the place where selections are typed as FEEL filters and applied.

b.text: Incorrect. Operate does not offer an Apply to all similar instances action on the incident banner; the supported flow is to build a selection from the list view.

c.text: Correct. The documented two-step flow is to check the box next to each instance that should be part of the selection and then choose the operation (retry or cancel) to apply.

d.text: Incorrect. Operate has no drag-and-drop target on the Operations panel; selections are built by check boxes on the list view and acted on through the operation controls.

explanation: Operate documents two simple steps for batch handling: check the box next to each process instance to include and then select the operation to apply. After confirmation, the Operations panel reports progress.
